content.full_text:
Assembly California Legislature

ROSS JOHNSON  
ASSEMBLYMAN, SIXTY-FOURTH DISTRICT  
ASSEMBLY REPUBLICAN LEADER

August 20, 1990

Mr. Scott Hallabrin  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Scott:

In recent conversations with staff you discussed the establishment of committees specifically designated for certain Assembly Districts, i.e. the 64th Republican Assembly District Committee. The purpose of such committee would be to register Republican voters, get-out-the-vote activity, and to promote Republican candidates in general.

You advised that officeholders and candidates cannot contribute to either the non-candidate or candidate support account of the 64th Republican Assembly District Committee, except Senators who overlap with the district. In that occurrence, such Senators could contribute to only the non-candidate support account for non-candidate specific voter registration and get-out-the-vote activity. In addition, those funds could not be used for an independent expenditure on behalf of or against a candidate.

You further indicated that the above-stated information is based on the assumption that the majority of the work done by the committee is in one district.

I would appreciate confirmation of this advice.

Sincerely,

Ross Johnson  
Assembly Republican Leader
